paper_title: Bee Pollen and Probiotics’ Potential to Protect and Treat Intestinal Permeability in Propionic Acid-Induced Rodent Model of Autism

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected data, focusing on oxidative stress markers and intestinal permeability biomarkers in a rodent model of autism.

steps.description: All data were carried out by one-way ANOVA followed by Tukey's multiple comparison test.
steps.searchable_summary: perform one-way ANOVA and Tukey's test on collected data

steps.description: Only p values ≤ 0.05 were considered significant.
steps.searchable_summary: filter results for significance with p ≤ 0.05

steps.description: Results illustrated as mean ± standard error of the mean (SEM) were obtained using GraphPad prism (version 9.5.0).
steps.searchable_summary: visualize results with mean ± SEM using GraphPad Prism

methods.description: Procedures for collecting, processing, and analyzing fecal specimens to identify microbial presence and quantify bacterial and fungal growth.

steps.description: The fecal specimens were weekly collected in the morning from all groups in sterile tubes and stored at −80°C.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and store fecal specimens at −80°C

steps.description: Frozen tubes were homogenized for 5 s in a vortex mixer and centrifuged (4000 rpm, 3 min, −4°C).
The obtained fecal suspensions were first subjected to tenfold serial dilutions with PBS solution.
steps.searchable_summary: perform tenfold serial dilutions on fecal suspensions

steps.description: An amount of 100 µL of each of the resulting dilutions was smeared on the surface of various culture media.
steps.searchable_summary: smear diluted samples on culture media

steps.description: MHA, MCA NA, and blood agar plates were incubated under aerobic conditions for 24 h at 37°C while SPS agar plates were incubated anaerobically for 24-48 h at 35 ± 2°C.
steps.searchable_summary: incubate culture plates under specified conditions

steps.description: Colony morphology based on size, color, and shape was evaluated microscopically on the microscope slide through the Gram staining method.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate colony morphology using Gram staining

steps.description: The data from the culture-based methods were quantified based on a ++++ scale defined as colony-forming unit (CFU).
Each isolate was characterized according to Holdeman et al. procedure.
steps.searchable_summary: characterize isolates using Holdeman et al. procedure
